question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all pending or recently gossiped transactions by iterating over sequential Hedera transaction IDs in the `/results/:transactionIdOrHash` route, using the 200/404 response differential to confirm which transaction IDs have been gossiped and which have not, causing transaction-level information leakage before finalization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user suppress knowledge of a gossiped transaction by sending a crafted `transactionIdOrHash` that matches a valid Ethereum hash format but resolves to a different transaction via `utils.parseHexStr()`, causing the mirror node to return a 404 and mislead downstream consumers about whether the transaction was gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user probe for the existence of a specific gossiped transaction by supplying a known transaction ID to `/results/:transactionIdOrHash/actions` and observing the 200/404 differential, effectively confirming gossip propagation of individual transactions without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all gossiped contract transactions within a specific block by supplying `block_number=N` to `/results`, causing the API to return the full set of gossiped transactions in that block and enabling an attacker to identify which transactions were included in gossip for that block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `block_hash` filter on `/results` to enumerate all gossiped transactions associated with a specific block hash, allowing reconstruction of the complete gossip payload for that block without any rate limiting or authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractContractResultsByIdQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the `transaction_nonce = 0` filter (which hides internal transactions) by setting `internal=true` in the query parameters, causing the API to expose all internal sub-transactions that were gossiped as part of a parent transaction, leaking the full gossip payload?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `nonce` query parameter in `getLastNonceParamValue()` to iterate over all nonce values for a given transaction ID, enumerating every duplicate or child transaction that was gossiped under that transaction ID, causing full gossip payload disclosure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a precise `consensusTimestamp` path parameter to `/results/:consensusTimestamp` to confirm whether a specific transaction was gossiped at that exact nanosecond timestamp, enabling timing-based gossip confirmation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the `/results` endpoint with high-frequency requests using different `timestamp` range filters to reconstruct the complete gossip timeline of all contract transactions, causing sustained database load that degrades the mirror node's ability to ingest and propagate gossip data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `transaction_hash` filter on `/results/logs` to confirm whether a specific Ethereum transaction was gossiped by observing the presence or absence of logs, effectively using the logs endpoint as a gossip confirmation oracle?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the dual-path resolution in `getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()` — where `utils.isValidEthHash()` determines whether to use hash or transaction ID lookup — to cause inconsistent gossip visibility by crafting an input that is ambiguously parsed, leading to one gossip path returning data while the other returns 404?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsById()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all gossiped transactions for a specific contract by paginating through `/contracts/:contractId/results` with no timestamp bounds, causing the API to return the complete gossip history for that contract without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `from` filter (sender address) on `/results` to enumerate all gossiped transactions originating from a specific account, enabling targeted gossip surveillance of a victim's transaction activity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: optimizeTimestampFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `optimizeTimestampFilters()` function's call to `bindTimestampRange()` by supplying an open-ended timestamp range, causing the function to perform an unbounded database scan that exhausts connection pool resources and prevents the mirror node from processing incoming gossip data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `/results/:transactionIdOrHash/actions` endpoint to retrieve the full internal call tree of a gossiped transaction, exposing all sub-calls and their parameters that were part of the gossip payload, without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractById()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the full bytecode of a contract (including `initcode` via `contract.initcode?.toString('hex')`) through `/contracts/:contractId`, exposing the deployment transaction's gossip payload content without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a partial content (HTTP 206) response by querying a transaction whose `callResult` is nil, and use this status code as a signal that the transaction was gossiped but not yet fully processed, enabling gossip state inference?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `hbar` query parameter in `getContractResultsByTimestamp()` to toggle HBAR value conversion and observe differences in returned data, using the discrepancy to infer gossip-level transaction value details?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractContractLogsMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `topic0`, `topic1`, `topic2`, `topic3` filters simultaneously to `/results/logs`, causing the multi-union query to generate an exponentially large SQL query that exhausts database resources and prevents gossip ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractLogsById()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `transaction_hash` filter on `/contracts/:contractId/results/logs` to confirm whether a specific transaction hash was gossiped and associated with a specific contract, using the log presence as a gossip confirmation oracle?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted Ethereum hash that collides with a valid transaction hash prefix to `/results/:transactionIdOrHash`, causing `ContractService.getContractTransactionDetailsByHash()` to return multiple results and exposing multiple gossiped transactions in a single response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user set `limit` to the maximum allowed value and combine it with `order=asc` and a very early `timestamp` to dump the entire gossip history of all contract transactions in bulk, enabling mass gossip data exfiltration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractStateById()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `timestamp` filter on `/contracts/:contractId/state` to retrieve the contract state at any historical point in time, effectively reconstructing the state changes caused by each gossiped transaction without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: alterTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with `timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:X` (same value) to trigger the `alterTimestampRange()` collapse into a single equality filter, causing the query to target a precise nanosecond timestamp and confirm whether a specific transaction was gossiped at that exact moment?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `transactionDetails.entityId = transactionDetails.payerAccountId` is set for non-hash lookups to infer the payer account of any gossiped transaction by querying `/results/:transactionId`, leaking gossip-level payer information?
